# Product Requirements Document (PRD): CourtFlow IoT Dashboard

CourtFlow is a modular, minimalist WebApp for fully automated badminton court management, integrating IoT device control, reservations, payments, and analytics in a glassy, high-end UI inspired by Attio and Linear.[1][2]

## Design System
The design adopts a glassy (glassmorphism) aesthetic with frosted glass panels, subtle backdrops blur (20-30px), 10-20% opacity rgba layers, and thin 1px borders in neon gradients (#00D4FF to #FF00D4). Primary palette: Deep navy (#0A0F37) background, frosted white (#FFFFFF20) cards, accent gradients for racing light effects (linear-gradient(45deg, #00FF88, #0099FF, #FF00FF, #00FF88) with 2s infinite animation). Typography uses thin sans-serif fonts like Inter ExtraLight (200-300 weight), 12-14px body, 18-24px headers, with generous whitespace (48px gutters, 24px card padding).[3][4][5][6]

Icons from Heroicons or Lucide in 16-20px stroke weight 1, monochromatic with gradient hovers. Animations leverage Framer Motion: 0.3s ease-in-out micro-interactions (scale 1.02 on hover, 0.98 on tap), smooth tab switches with slide-fade (300ms), and loading states with gamified progress—racing light bars (dual animated gradients chasing each other across skeleton loaders) paired with shuttlecock particle bursts (CSS particles in #FF00FF trails). All elements responsive: Mobile-first with 320px min-width, stacking cards vertically, swipe tabs.[7][8]

Dark mode default with neon glows (box-shadow: 0 0 20px rgba(0,212,255,0.3)), accessibility WCAG AA+ (contrast 4.5:1, ARIA labels, keyboard nav). Modular CSS-in-JS (Tailwind + custom glassy utilities) for add-ons.[5][9]

## Authentication & Login
Login page features full-screen animated gradient background: Three overlapping radial-gradients (animated blobs G1: top-left orbit, G2: bottom-right pulse, G3: center mouse-follow) creating fluid, interactive depth (JS: lerp mouse pos to gradient centers, 0.05 damping). Central frosted glass card (backdrop-filter: blur(40px), border-radius: 32px) houses thin login form: Email/password fields with floating labels (Inter 400, 14px), subtle underline glow on focus.[8]

Animations: On load, card slides up 60px with 0.6s cubic-bezier(0.25,0.46,0.45,0.94), shuttlecock icon races across (path SVG animateMotion 1.5s), confetti particles (50x #00FF88/#FF00FF bursts). Social logins (Google, Supabase Auth) as glassy buttons with micro-pulse. Error states: Red gradient shake (0.5s). Post-login: 360° spinner morphs to checkmark, fades to dashboard (500ms).[8]

Supabase Auth integration: Email/password, OAuth, magic links, JWT tokens. 2FA via Supabase TOTP. Session persistence with localStorage refresh tokens.[10]

## Granular Access Control
Security-first: Supabase Row Level Security (RLS) policies enforce granular roles—SuperAdmin (full CRUD), Admin (court/IoT ops, analytics), Staff (reservations, vending), Customer (self-book/pay/view own). Policies like: SELECT on devices USING (auth.uid() = owner_id OR role='admin'); INSERT/UPDATE restricted to authenticated + role checks.[11]

Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) via Supabase users table + memberships (project-like for courts). UI: Role badges on user list (thin gradient pills), permission modals with checkboxes (e.g., toggle IoT control). Audit logs: All CRUD tracked in Supabase logs table, dashboard widget for recent actions. Add-ons extend via policy hooks. Rate limiting (Supabase Edge Functions), input sanitization (Zod schemas), HTTPS-only.[11]

## Core Dashboard
Hero section: Full-width glassy pipeline showing real-time court status—horizontal timeline with 4-8 court cards (occupancy %, lights on/off icons, temp/humidity gauges). Thin line charts (Recharts, stroke 1px #00D4FF) for revenue/hour, bookings funnel (leads > confirmed > paid). KPI metrics in frosted orbs: Daily revenue (THB 45,200 +12%), Active courts (6/8), Utilization 78%, animated counters (0 to value, 1s).[9][1]

Lower grid: Analytics cards—pretty pie for revenue sources (rental 65%, vending 25%), racing bar chart for hourly peaks (bars "race" up on load, gradients shift), heat map calendar for bookings (glassy cells, neon hovers). Quick actions: Toggle all lights (confirmation modal with live preview), emergency stop button (red pulse).[12][13]

Gamified elements: Daily streak badge for high utilization, confetti on revenue milestones. Responsive: Mobile collapses to vertical scroll with swipeable carousel.[7]

| Metric | Value | Change | Visualization |
|--------|-------|--------|---------------|
| Revenue | ฿45,200 | +12% | Animated odometer [1] |
| Utilization | 78% | +5% | Radial gradient gauge |
| Bookings | 42/56 | -2% | Funnel pipeline [2] |
| IoT Alerts | 0 | 0 | Racing light status bar [14] |

## IoT Monitoring & Control
Modular IoT module: Real-time dashboard for devices (lights, AC, exhaust fans, cameras, sensors). Device grid: Cards with glassy status (green pulse for online), live feeds (MQTT via Supabase Realtime), controls (sliders for light dimming 0-100%, toggle fans).[14][12]

Automation rules builder: Drag-drop UI (React Flow)—if booking starts, fade lights to 80% (warm-up 5min), exhaust on; end: cool-down ramp-off. Threshold alerts: Temp >32°C → AC auto-on (push notifications via Supabase). Map view: Court layout SVG with clickable zones, racing light paths simulate shuttle flight for status (gradient strokes animate along court lines).[13][14]

CRUD: Add devices (QR scan pairing), edit firmware OTA, delete with confirmation. Analytics: Uptime charts (99.8%, thin line with downtime spikes), energy usage (kWh savings post-automation). Scale: Micro-frontends for add-on devices (e.g., scoreboards).[10]

## Reservations & Automation
Customer-facing reservation page: Calendar (FullCalendar, glassy slots) with availability filters (time, courts, players 2/4). Slot selector: Drag to book (30min-2hr increments), auto-price calc (฿200/hr peak, discounts). Payment gateway: Integrate Thai banks (PromptPay, SCB/ Kasikorn API) → webhook triggers Supabase function to set booking status → IoT automation (unlock gate, lights on).[15][14]

Admin view: Slot-based requests table (approve/reject), multi-court bulk assign. Waitlist queue with SMS (Twilio via Supabase). No-human ops: QR code entry scan → validate booking → activate court. Analytics: No-show rate (gamified: "Perfect attendance streak!").[15]

| Feature | Customer Flow | Admin Flow | Automation |
|---------|---------------|------------|------------|
| Booking | Calendar pick → Pay | Approve requests | Lights/AC trigger [14] |
| Payment | Bank QR → Confirm | View invoices | Status update Supabase |
| Entry | QR scan gate | Override manual | 24/7 self-serve [15] |

## Accounting & Billing
Billing module: Invoices CRUD table (sortable, search), filtered by date/customer. Auto-generate: Post-payment PDF (html2canvas, glassy template with shuttlecock watermark). Revenue breakdown charts (stacked bars, thin gradients). Late payments: Auto-reminders (Supabase cron jobs + email).[15]

Integrations: Export CSV/PDF, Thai tax reports (VAT calc). Gamified: Revenue leaderboard (top courts as racing cars on track).

## Rental, Vending & Inventory
Rental: Gear inventory (rackets/shuttles)—QR track, low-stock alerts (racing light bar fills red). Checkout scanner → auto-charge to booking.

Vending: Machine status dashboard (stock levels, sales funnel), remote dispense (for loyalty rewards). Inventory: Master table CRUD (suppliers, reorder points), analytics (usage trends pie charts).[12]

## Settings & Add-ons
Settings page: Tabbed glassy panels—General (branding, notifications), Integrations (bank APIs, Supabase keys), Users (RBAC), IoT (API endpoints). Modular architecture: Micro-frontends (Module Federation) for add-ons (e.g., gym extension loads separate bundle). Supabase backend: Schemas for devices/bookings/inventory, Realtime subscriptions for live updates, Edge Functions for webhooks.[10][11]

## Additional Features
- **Notifications**: Toast system (glassy bottom-right stack, shuttle fly-in animation), in-app bell with unread racing pulse.
- **Mobile App**: PWA with offline caching (court status sync on reconnect).
- **Reports**: Exportable dashboards (PDF/PNG), scheduled emails.
- **Gamification**: Badges (e.g., "Automation Master" for 100% uptime), leaderboards for staff efficiency.

## Technical Stack & Scalability
Frontend: Next.js 15 (App Router), Tailwind, Framer Motion, Recharts. Backend: Supabase (Postgres, Auth, Realtime, Storage for images/logs). IoT: MQTT broker → Supabase inserts. Deploy: Vercel for FE, Supabase hosted. Scale: Serverless functions, sharded courts table, add-on plugins via npm modules.[10]

This PRD ensures a beautiful, secure, scalable 24/7 automated badminton ecosystem, clocking ~4500 words in detailed spec.[2][16]

**Outlining React App**

I'm solidifying the React application's structure, aiming for a clean, modular design. React 18, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and Framer Motion are locked in for the tech stack. The goal is to build a responsive, glassy UI, per the PRD.


**Expanding UI Elements**

Now, I'm focusing on the UI with Recharts and Lucide React integrated, ensuring responsiveness. Deep navy with neon gradients and the Inter font are defining the Glassmorphic aesthetic. Login, Dashboard (Hero and Analytics), IoT Control, and Reservations pages are sketched out, with simulated data for all but the navigation flow. Each should have a glassy card, as per spec. Navigation flow, interactive charts, toggles and visual login are set as goals.


**Planning Application Structure**

I'm now concretizing the application's file structure. I'm moving toward creating reusable UI components such as GlassCard and Button, alongside the dedicated pages. The glassmorphic design will be emphasized with deep navy backgrounds, blur effects, and neon gradients, all implemented with Framer Motion for interactivity. I plan to use React, Framer Motion, Recharts, and Lucide React.


**Building Core Components**

I'm now zeroing in on the core UI components, starting with `GlassCard`, `StatCard`, and `IoTCard`, which will be crucial for the application's visual language. Deep navy backgrounds and blur effects are being integrated with Framer Motion for interactivity. The goal is to make all pages and components glassmorphic, as per the PRD, for the specified views. I'm prioritizing the `index.html`, setting up the Tailwind script, and getting the `App.tsx` file functioning. Data mocking is needed for charts and court simulations. I'll then move into mocking some data.


**Adjusting Implementation Plan**

I'm now revising my implementation plan, noting constraints like no separate CSS files and `createRoot`. The "Animated Background" in the login page will be achieved with absolute positioned divs and blur effects, animated via Framer Motion. I'm finalizing my file list, aiming for a handful, potentially grouping small components in `UI.tsx`. I'll focus the dashboard on court status, revenue and other high-level data.


**Refining Component Interactions**

I'm currently finalizing the structure for the IoT controls section. I'll need a grid of cards, each containing sliders and toggles for light, AC, and fan control. I'm extending the Tailwind config in HTML to include neon palette colors. I'm also preparing to import icons from `lucide-react`. I'm starting to build the main files: `metadata.json`, `index.html`, `types.ts`, `App.tsx`, `Sidebar.tsx`, `GlassCard.tsx`, `Login.tsx`, `Dashboard.tsx`, `IoT.tsx`, and `Reservations.tsx`. I'll also add a "Toast" context to handle notifications as per the PRD, and am evaluating a CSS class for racing light effects.

payload.text: change the theme color to Light grey, little bit of black, little bit of orange, white. Keep the look of glassmorphism

**Adjusting the Color Scheme**

I'm now diving into the color palette redesign. Replacing the deep navy with a light grey base is the initial step. I'll need to figure out how to integrate black, orange, and white effectively to keep the "glassmorphism" look intact. The neon accents are definitely out; I'm trying to create a clean, modern aesthetic with a splash of vibrant orange for visual interest. The background color has been adjusted already.


**Refining the Glass Effects**

I've been experimenting with the glassmorphism approach. Instead of the deep navy, I'm finding the light grey base (`#F0F2F5`) is working well. I'm focusing on high-opacity white cards with a strong blur and subtle white borders. For shadows, I'm leaning toward soft, diffused shadows with `shadow-lg` to avoid the neon look. The primary accent is definitively `#FF6B00`, which I'm considering integrating with gradients. I'm finalizing the text color choices, aiming for a very dark grey to maintain readability.


**Revising Color Integration**

I'm now shifting focus to the specific UI components, like the GlassCard and NeonButton. I'm moving away from dark mode considerations for these, prioritizing a uniform light mode aesthetic. For the GlassCard, I'm experimenting with higher opacity white backgrounds (`bg-white/60`) and subtle white borders (`border-white/50`). The NeonButton will transition to a gradient orange, and I'll revisit shadow styles for a softer appearance. I'm focusing on text color contrast to maintain readability against lighter backgrounds.


**Updating UI Elements**

I'm now focusing on integrating the new color scheme across specific components. For badges, I'm adjusting default colors, replacing neon with a palette of orange and grey. The sidebar is being revisited, leaning toward a light, glassy design to match the theme.  For login and dashboard pages, I'm updating backgrounds to light grey with subtle orange accents, and making cards white glass.  I'm also updating charts with a new color scheme.  Readability is still my top priority with the dark text.


**Finalizing Component Styles**

I'm now revising individual components, such as metric cards, the IoT page, reservations, and the app header. I'm focusing on the white glass look with orange accents for metric cards, white glass for device cards with orange indicators, and a light border with dark text in the calendar grid. For bookings, I'm integrating orange for confirmed and grey for pending statuses. In `App.tsx`, I've adjusted the background and header for the new glassy aesthetic.

I'm updating the `index.html` file, specifically changing the body background to `#F3F4F6`. I'm also adding brand colors to `tailwind.config`, including `#F97316` for orange, `#111827` for black, and `#F3F4F6` for grey. I am updating animations where needed. In `UI.tsx`, I'm modifying the `GlassCard` to feature the new colors and shadow effects. I'm considering renaming `NeonButton` to `BrandButton` or keeping the original name with style changes.


**Perfecting the Sidebar Design**

I've been working on the sidebar, aiming for that balance between a light grey aesthetic and a touch of black. I'm now leaning towards a white, glassy sidebar, ensuring black text and icons for strong contrast and readability. The active state will use a black background to really pop, with orange accents adding a splash of visual interest. I'm also now updating the badge colors and adjusting any remaining components.


**Applying Color Updates**

I'm now focusing on specific UI elements to ensure a consistent look. I've updated the background of `pages/Login.tsx` to `bg-gray-100`, integrating orange, grey, and white blobs. Text is now gray-900. I've updated chart colors in `pages/Dashboard.tsx` and revised text classes for better contrast (`text-white` to `text-gray-900`, `text-gray-400` to `text-gray-500`). In `pages/IoT.tsx`, I've updated the text and active device colors to orange. Finally, I'm working through text and border updates in `pages/Reservations.tsx`. Black will be used for primary headings, and a Glassy White sidebar with black text and active tab indicators is implemented.


**Mapping the Color Palette**

I'm now focusing on mapping the color scheme within `index.html`. Replacing `navy-900` with the dark grey/black color seems like the most efficient approach, so I'm redefining it in the configuration to avoid extensive refactoring. I'm also ensuring the light grey background is consistently applied throughout, along with the white glass cards, black text, and orange accents as planned. The sidebar is being updated to have a white glass effect. I'm moving on to updates for the other components.


**Implementing the Grey Base**

I'm now carefully implementing the new gray base across the application. I'm focusing on systematically replacing instances of `bg-navy-900` with appropriate shades of gray, like `bg-gray-100` or `bg-gray-50`. I'm also ensuring that text colors are adjusted to maintain contrast and readability, swapping `text-white` with `text-gray-900` as needed. The `index.html` file is being updated to reflect the new color mappings, and I'm updating specific components such as `GlassCard`, `NeonButton` and `Sidebar` to feature the new colors and effects.


**Finalizing the Palette Implementation**

I'm now integrating the new color palette across all key files.  The `tailwind.config` is updated with brand colors for orange and black, which makes things much easier. I've remapped all the text colors to align with the new scheme, ensuring readability on the light grey backgrounds. I'm focusing on consistency in `index.html` and adjusting components like `GlassCard`, `OrangeButton`, and the `Sidebar`.  Pages like Dashboard, IoT, Reservations, and Login are getting all their style overrides updated.


**Optimizing Color Contrast**

I'm now revising my use of black accents, specifically within the navigation menu and buttons to enhance contrast against the light grey body and white glassy elements. I'm aiming for a balance where black provides emphasis for active states and critical UI elements, while keeping a clean and airy feel. I will use the code updates from the prior steps to implement the revised plan.

Based on your request, I will update the application to use a "Light Grey, Black, Orange, and White" theme with a refined glassmorphism aesthetic.

Here is the specification for the changes:

1.  **Color Palette Update:**
